reasoning: Iteration 0 achieved excellent performance (89.4 ms, within 3.5% of DuckDB's best) but fails validation due to floating-point precision errors. Critical fix required: Kahan summation for compensated accumulation to ensure deterministic results regardless of parallel thread order. After fixing correctness, apply high-impact performance optimizations (SIMD date filtering + zone map skipping) to close the final 3.5% gap and potentially exceed DuckDB's speed.
focus_areas: Fix floating-point accumulation precision using Kahan summation algorithm
Apply SIMD vectorized date filtering for 15-22% scan_filter speedup
Add zone map block skipping for additional 2-4% gains on sorted shipdate data
strategy_notes: CRITICAL: Select recommendation 0 (Kahan summation - semantic/critical_fix) FIRST and exclusively. This must pass validation before attempting performance optimizations. Once correctness is established, apply recommendations 1 (Zone Map Block Skipping - priority 1, low risk, io_bound) and 2 (SIMD Vectorized Date Filtering - priority 2, medium risk, cpu_bound) together. These are complementary: zone maps reduce data volume, SIMD accelerates comparisons on remaining data. scan_filter dominates at 60.5% of time, so these optimizations directly target the bottleneck. Target: bring total time to ~75-80 ms range to exceed DuckDB's performance while maintaining correctness.
notes: Recommendation 0 is the critical fix (Kahan summation) - MUST be applied. Recommendations 1 and 2 are performance optimizations to apply after correctness is restored. The Query Optimizer can handle all three simultaneously since 0 is semantic/correctness and 1-2 are different optimization techniques. Do not apply performance optimizations if the critical fix fails.
